name: Rocha dos Soídos Loop from Alte
name_original: A Rocha dos Soidos partindo de Alte — percurso circular
description: This moderate 10.8-mile (17.3 km) road cycling route from Alte features the challenging Alto dos Soidos climb and scenic Algarve views.
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes around Alte are characterized by a diverse landscape at the foot of the Serra do Caldeirão, transitioning between the fertile Barrocal region and mountainous terrain. The area features hilly landscapes, quiet mountain roads, and stretches of countryside dotted with olive and citrus groves. This provides varied and scenic backdrops for road cyclists seeking challenging climbs and descents away from busy traffic. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Alte The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Bike Café - Germano – Orange Grove Near Alte loop from Alte, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This route navigates through scenic agricultural areas and quiet country lanes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto do Malhão – Bike Café - Germano loop from Alte, a moderate 20.4 miles (32.8 km) path. This route includes the challenging Alto do Malhão climb, offering demanding ascents and panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Bike Café - Germano – Orange Grove Near Alte loop from Alte, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through quiet roads and orange groves, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Road cycling around Alte is defined by hilly landscapes, quiet mountain roads, and olive and citrus groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to routes incorporating significant climbs. The routes in Alte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available around Alte?
A: There are 15 dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes around Alte, offering a variety of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes are designed to keep you away from busy roads, allowing for a peaceful ride through the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these no-traffic road cycling routes in Alte?
A: The terrain around Alte is diverse, transitioning from the fertile Barrocal region to the hilly and mountainous Serra do Caldeirão. You can expect a mix of rolling hills, demanding climbs, and scenic descents, providing a good challenge for road cyclists. The routes often pass through rugged countryside, olive and citrus groves, and along quiet mountain roads.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs on the no-traffic road cycling routes near Alte?
A: Yes, Alte is known for its challenging climbs. The most famous is the &lt;strong&gt;Alto do Malhão&lt;/strong&gt;, an iconic ascent often featured in professional races like the Volta ao Algarve. You can experience this climb on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32267540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Malhão – Bike Café - Germano loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular no-traffic road cycling routes starting from Alte?
A: Absolutely. All the no-traffic road cycling routes listed for Alte are circular loops, meaning you'll start and finish in the same location. This makes planning your ride convenient, as you won't need to arrange transport back to your starting point.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the no-traffic road cycling routes in Alte?
A: Many routes will take you past stunning natural features. You might encounter the refreshing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/568312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Grande de Alte&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful natural pool, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/460116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrocal Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The unique limestone formation of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent landmark in the area, offering geological interest and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any cafes or amenities catering to cyclists on these quiet routes?
A: Yes, Alte is becoming increasingly cycling-friendly. Establishments like "Bike Café - Germano" cater specifically to cyclists, offering a convenient stop for coffee or recovery. Several routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14595210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Café - Germano – Orange Grove Near Alte loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate this popular spot.

Q: What is the best time of year to enjoy no-traffic road cycling in Alte?
A: The Algarve generally enjoys mild winters and warm springs and autumns, making these seasons ideal for road cycling. Avoiding the peak heat of summer (July-August) will ensure a more comfortable experience, especially on routes with significant climbs. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly no-traffic road cycling routes in Alte?
A: While many routes in Alte are moderate to difficult due to the hilly terrain, there is at least one easy route available. These easier options typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a gentler introduction to road cycling in the region without heavy traffic.

Q: Where can I park my car to access these no-traffic road cycling routes in Alte?
A: Alte village itself offers various parking options, particularly near the village center or close to popular starting points for routes. Since many routes are loops starting from Alte, you can typically find convenient parking within the village before heading out on your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Alte?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Alte, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's quiet mountain roads, the stunning panoramic views, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs like the Alto do Malhão. The authentic Portuguese charm of the area, away from coastal crowds, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through orange groves or other scenic agricultural areas?
A: Yes, the region's fertile Barrocal area is characterized by olive and citrus groves. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21835258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orange Grove Near Alte – Fonte Grande de Alte loop from Alte&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight these picturesque agricultural landscapes, offering a beautiful and aromatic cycling experience.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the no-traffic road cycling routes in Alte?
A: The hilly and mountainous terrain naturally provides numerous opportunities for stunning views. You can find designated viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6382157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cerro do Malhão and the Mountains&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6378366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Grande Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt; also offers expansive panoramic vistas of the Algarve countryside.
tips: This is a bike café dedicated exclusively to cyclists, in Alte (Loulé). It is a mandatory meeting point for cyclists traveling the roads and paths of the Algarve coast, as well as a stop to rest and sample typical cuisine.

Quiet village, with cobbled streets, fireplaces, springs and the beautiful Queda do Vigário waterfall. In summer, the river beaches of Fonte Pequena and Fonte Grande, which are, in reality, bathing basins on the Alte river, are very pleasant.
The rock massif of Rocha dos Soidos rises about 10 km northeast of Alte. The 467 m high cliffs fall on the north side so the most beautiful view of the massif is from the north side.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "moderate road ride" and requires good fitness due to significant elevation changes. The Rocha dos Soidos climb, in particular, has a challenging average gradient of 9.9% over 1.6 km. It is not recommended for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for road bikes. You will encounter significant elevation changes, including the challenging Alto dos Soidos climb. While generally good, some regional routes can have unpaved sections, but this specific route is largely described as having good road conditions.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 17.3 km circular route is around 55 minutes, though this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque views of the inland Algarve, winding through rugged countryside and olive groves. Key highlights include the Rocha dos Soidos massif itself, which provides beautiful vistas, especially from its north side. The starting point, Alte, is a charming traditional village with whitewashed buildings and cobblestone streets, and features like Fonte Grande and Fonte Pequena.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route in Alte?
faqs.answer: Alte is a popular starting point for cyclists, and parking is generally available within the village. Look for designated parking areas, especially near the village center, to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Algarve region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, providing milder weather and lush landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, bringing dogs along is generally not recommended for their safety and the safety of cyclists, especially given the traffic on roads and the challenging climbs. It's best to leave pets at home for this particular ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It uses public roads and natural areas that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink in Alte before or after the ride?
faqs.answer: Yes, Alte is well-equipped with amenities. It is home to the famous Bike Café - Germano, which is a recommended stop for cyclists. You'll find other cafes and restaurants in the charming village center for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. Approximately 18% of the route overlaps with PR15 - Entre o Barrocal e a Serra, 16% with the Via Algarviana, and 14% with Via Algarviana - Ligação 7: Albufeira-Alte.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for tackling the Alto dos Soidos climb?
faqs.answer: The Alto dos Soidos climb is a significant challenge, spanning 1.6 km with an average gradient of 9.9%. It's advisable to conserve energy before this section and be prepared for a sustained effort. Ensure your bike gearing is suitable for steep ascents. The reward is the stunning views from the Rocha dos Soidos massif at the top.
